task_completion.description: Open the Chart of Accounts or Account Element window
task_completion.visual_evidence: The iDempiere application shows the 'Account Element' or 'Chart of Accounts' window active on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the Search Key and Name for the new account
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Search Key' field contains '76500' and the 'Name' field contains 'Social Media Advertising'.

task_completion.description: Enter the Description for the new account
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Description' field contains 'Expenses for social media advertising campaigns'.

task_completion.description: Set the Account Type to Expense
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Account Type' dropdown or field is set to 'Expense' (or 'E').

task_completion.description: Set the Account Sign to Natural and Summary to No
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Account Sign' dropdown or field is set to 'Natural' (or 'N') and the 'Summary' checkbox is unchecked or set to 'No' (or 'N').
A success message is displayed, the save button is grayed out/inactive, or the new account appears in the list view of the Chart of Accounts.

integrity.description: Agent used the iDempiere GUI to create the account
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web-based iDempiere interface, not terminal commands or database queries.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are entered into the iDempiere form fields, not a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: Results appear to come from genuine software interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen shows the standard iDempiere layout, menus, and toolbars consistent with creating a new record.

privileged_info_for_vlm: In iDempiere, Account Type 'Expense' corresponds to the internal code 'E', Account Sign 'Natural' corresponds to 'N', and Summary 'No' corresponds to 'N' (or an unchecked checkbox). The VLM should accept either the display names ('Expense', 'Natural', 'No') or their internal codes ('E', 'N', 'N'/unchecked) as correct visual evidence.
